title: glechoma humilis
intro: Glechoma humilis, also known as Creeping Lemon or Creeping Spurred Vetch, is a perennial plant species native to Asia and Europe. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its essential oils, which contain compounds such as borneol, borneol acetate, and limonene, among others. The health benefits of Glechoma humilis include its use as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic agent, which can help to alleviate digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation. Glechoma humilis can be consumed as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
